name: Ken's Family Living Room Doorway
location: Ken's family apartment in a high-rise building in Ginza, Tokyo, Japan
layout: Spacious yet restrained open-plan living room focused on the doorway area; low-profile tea table positioned centrally near the deep-colored sofa against one wall; wooden flooring extends seamlessly; light gray walls create subtle layers; doorway frames the entry from a hallway, with minimal furnishings emphasizing empty space around the threshold
materials: Polished wooden flooring with subtle sheen; light gray matte walls; dark fabric-upholstered sofa with leather accents; low wooden tea table with natural grain textures; sheer curtains on windows for diffused light filtering
lighting: Natural daylight filtered through sheer curtains on large windows, casting soft, mottled shadows on the floor and furniture edges; subtle ambient glow from urban exterior at dusk, avoiding harsh contrasts
atmosphere: Clean and slightly cool air with a sense of restrained suspense; acoustic absorption by walls and fabrics muffling distant city hum; realistic cinematic depth with clear textural distinctions between wood, fabric, and leather
era_region: Modern urban Japan, Tokyo Ginza district
establishing_prompt: Empty modern Japanese living room doorway in a Ginza apartment, spacious with restrained furnishings: low wooden tea table, deep sofa against light gray wall, polished wood floor with subtle reflections, sheer curtains on windows diffusing soft natural light into mottled shadows, clean and cool atmosphere, realistic cinematic style, no people or props
